questions.question_text: Why did our readmission rate drop 23% below what we're reporting?
questions.answer_summary: Seven metrics across three dashboards use inconsistent denominator definitions. The ED dashboard excludes observation stays while the quality report includes them, creating a 23-point gap that overstates performance to CMS.

questions.question_text: Are we actually meeting our sepsis bundle compliance target?
questions.answer_summary: The compliance dashboard double-counts patients who transfer between units, inflating the numerator by 14%. True compliance is 71%, not the 85% shown in board reports.

questions.question_text: Why does cardiology show 40% higher revenue per case than the regional benchmark?
questions.answer_summary: Charge capture for interventional procedures includes bundled supplies that peer hospitals bill separately. Apples-to-apples comparison shows revenue is 6% above benchmark, not 40%.

questions.question_text: How reliable is our department-level cost-per-patient metric?
questions.answer_summary: Overhead allocation uses a 2019 cost model that hasn't been updated for post-COVID volume shifts. Three departments carry $2.1M in misallocated indirect costs, distorting unit economics.

questions.question_text: Can we trust the clinical quality scores we're sending to payers?
questions.answer_summary: Two of five quality measures pull from staging tables that lag production by 48 hours. During month-end reconciliation windows, scores can shift by up to 9 points before final submission.
